title: Python dodag.py: hysteresis logic change may break backward compatibility
description:
The hysteresis logic in DodagState.select_parent() was changed from:
  'if best_cost > current_cost - threshold' (switch only if best is significantly better)
to:
  'improvement = current_cost - best_cost; if improvement < threshold' (switch if improvement exceeds threshold)

These are mathematically equivalent when best_cost < current_cost, but the new form handles the case where best_cost >= current_cost differently. This is actually a bug fix (the old form could switch when best was worse), but existing tests should be verified to ensure behavior matches expectations.

File: python/src/lichen/rpl/dodag.py lines 153-156
close_reason: Tests verify behavior - test_does_not_switch_without_meaningful_improvement and test_switches_on_large_improvement pass
